title: Real-Time Q-PCR Protocol for Chicken Sex Identification
author: Liyan He
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ugaetse

steps.title: Put 150 µl of lysis buffer in each 1
steps.instruction: Put 150 µl of lysis buffer in each 1.5 ml tube or well of 96-well plate for each sample to analyze.

steps.title: Pipet 10~20 mg of embryo tissues...
steps.instruction: Pipet 10~20 mg of embryo tissues from the embryo head (brain), membrane, chorion or other into the tube/well containing the lysis buffer.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 µl proteinase K at 20 mg/ml stock into each tube/well and vortex 3 seconds.

steps.title: Incubate the tubes/plate in the thermocycler
steps.instruction: Incubate the tubes/plate in the thermocycler. 03:15:00 Program: · Heat lid at 60°C · Incubate 3h at 55°C · Incubate 15 min at 95°C · Hold at 4°C

steps.title: Centrifuge 5 minutes at 13000 rpm...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 minutes at 13000 rpm (or 15 mins at 8000 rpm) at room temperature. 00:05:00

steps.title: Pipet 50 µl-100 µl of the supernatant
steps.instruction: Pipet 50 µl-100 µl of the supernatant. Put it in a new 96-well plate (this primary stock solution contains the template DNA at a concentration between 500 and 1000 ng/µl per tube) and store it: - at 4°C if you are doing the PCR the same day. - at -20°C if you are going to do the PCR another day (DNA is degraded with time in biological extracts).

steps.title: Dilute the supernatant 4 times into...
steps.instruction: Dilute the supernatant 4 times into a new 96-well plate (For example add 25 µl of the supernatant to 75 µl of nuclease free water). It will give a final concentration of prepared template DNA between 125 and 250 ng/µl.

steps.title: Two set of primers (Xho-I &...
steps.instruction: Two set of primers (Xho-I & DMRT) are used in this protocol. Xho-I Primers Xho-I Forward: CCCAAATATAACACGCTTCACT Xho-I Reverse: GAAATGAATTATTTTCTGGCGAC DMRT Primers DMRT-57 Forward: CTTTCACAAATGTGTTCTGCTGT DMRT-57 Reverse: AGCAGATACAACCTAAGAATGCC Prepare a 10 µM primer working solution from 100 μM primer stock.

steps.title: 1 . Assemble all reaction components on ice in this order
steps.instruction: 1 . Assemble all reaction components on ice in this order: %s %s 2. Seal the plate, vortex and centrifuge briefly. 3. Transfer the Q-PCR plate into the LightCycler® 480 system. In the lab we use the LightCycler® 480 System and the LightCycler® 480 SYBR GREEN I master reaction mix (Roche, Bâle, Swiss). It’s advised to use the 384 q-PCR plate to perform the reaction with 10 µl per well.

steps.title: The following table shows the PCR...
steps.instruction: The following table shows the PCR parameters set on a LightCycler ® 480 system PCR run using a 384 multiwell plate. %s %s

steps.title: Crossing Point Analysis (Cp)
steps.instruction: Crossing Point Analysis (Cp): Average Cp was 8.6 for females, 21.1 for males and 32 for negative control. By defining a Cp value cut-off at 18, the assay is able to discriminate between males and females with 100% concordance. A Cp difference of more than 10 cycles between female and male samples containing between 1 ng and 1 μg DNA is observed. It makes it suitable for sex determination without DNA quantification. As a second step, to reinforce the Cp discrimination, the melting temperature (tm) profiles need to be compared.

steps.title: Melting Curve Analysis (Tm)
steps.instruction: Melting Curve Analysis (Tm): Female chicken exhibit one single peak around 80°C, whereas males show two peaks around 75°C and 84°C. To discriminate males and females chicken, use the ratio of the intensities between 84°C and 80°C. The averages Tm ratios of 84°C/80°C intensities are 0.033 ±0.1 for females and 1.76 ±0.5 for males. A 84°C/80°C ratio cut-off at 0.33 lead to 100% of discrimination between males and females.
